feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Adding columns to an active Replication Flow (S/4HANA to S3) without losing CDC Delta pointer
entry.content:
Hi all,

We currently have a Replication Flow in SAP Datasphere moving data from S/4HANA to an Amazon S3 bucket using the "Initial and Delta" load type.

The source is a custom CDS View with CDC annotations that joins two SAP tables (EKKO/EKPO) and currently has 84 columns.

The question is: If we need to add new columns to this CDS View in the future, is there a way to incorporate them into the existing Replication Flow without triggering a full Re-initialization (Full Load)?

We want to avoid losing the current Delta pointer (subscription) in ODP/ODQ, as a full reload of these tables would be highly time-consuming due to the volume of data. Is there any "Schema Evolution" support or a best practice to handle this "on-the-fly"?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Delta enabled replication Options in SAP Datasphere with the focus on using SAP extractors 2LIS
entry.content:
Hi,

Could someone please help me with an issue? I am extracting data from an S/4 system using 2LIS extractors. I have completed all prerequisites, including enabling the primary keys. However, when I try to run the delta-enabled replication, I am getting an error.

Could anyone please guide me on how to resolve this and successfully achieve the required data load?

please find the error attached

entry.title: Customer influence - please vote for ECC on HANA connector for Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi,

for our SAP ECC environment (ECC on HANA with S4 Finance 1605 activated), 70% of our extraction to the current BW platform is done with abap CDS views via ODP. This is currently not possible in Datasphere/BDC. As a result, we cannot develop most of our usecases on BDC to take advantage of the databricks delta share.

We want to innovate in BDC/Databricks, but also on data from "older" systems.

If you have similar issues, please vote for https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/373140.

If we have enough votes, SAP might want to consider fixing this.

Thanks,

Christophe Posson

entry.title: SAC and Datasphere to use same Idp for SSO
entry.content:
Hi,

We are looking into setting up Seamless Planning and also reporting on a tunnel connection in SAC with data from Datasphere.

Can see that we will have to use the same IdP to make it happen, so how do we get the possibility in Datasphere to make the same IdP as in SAC.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3720272

 

Best regards

Jesper Bonde

entry.title: Cloud Connector Setup for Datasphere Connection
entry.content:
I am currently configuring the connectivity between SAP Cloud Connector and an SAP Datasphere tenant.
The subaccount has already been successfully added to the Cloud Connector, and the on‑premise systems that need to be accessed have also been configured.

However, I am unclear about which specific resources must be defined and exposed for each on‑premise system (for example: paths, protocols, ports, and/or required resources) in order for SAP Datasphere to access them correctly through the Cloud Connector.

I would appreciate your support in validating which resources are required depending on the type of on‑premise system, and in confirming whether there are any recommended configurations or official documentation for this scenario.

entry.title: Missing Association After Data Product Deployment in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

A Sales table is modeled as a fact view with an association to a Product dimension (modeled as a separate graphical view). Both views are exposed for consumption. A data product is created on top of these two artifacts - Sales and Product and deployed. And the data product is installed in the target space while both views are available in the target space, the association is missing. Is this by design.

Could you help clarify why this happens. Thanks!
--
KRPK

entry.title: Replication flow and SAP conversion routine
entry.content:
Hello all

I'm facing with a little problem and a I would like see if you guys already know it and maybe any kind of solution.

The problem:

I have replication flow configured getting data from SAP-ECC system. It's been working fine but for some tables that have data element with conversion routine (Eg: T001 LANGU) Datasphere only consider external format I mean in DB table we have LANG as E (English) but external format is (EN) . 

I have some scenarios that must be just one character (w/o conversion routine) such as in DB.

Does any one had the same problem ?

Although i have an workaround in track but I'm trying look for options (I mean more clear solutions).

Thanks in Advance

entry.title: How to replicate FAGLFLEXT from SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
We are currently migrating our SAP ECC to S4 HANA.

We used to replicate data to SAP DSP via SAP SLT. We know many tables are changing so we are leveraging the ingestion of CDS views to fix some replication issues.

However, we are struggling to ingest FAGLFLEXT into SAP DSP. The CDS view created on top of compatibility view has all the OBJNR00~08 key field set to 0 (for the reason detailed here: 3380889 - Object number fields OBJNR00~08 is 0 in FAGLFLEXT after upgrade to S/4 HANA - SAP for Me). So, when we load te data from the CDS into SAP DSP, the system is working as a SELECT DISTINCT on the key because out of millions of records displayed in the source system, we replicate only few records (the ones with the Primary Key distinct).

We are using the FAGLFLEXT downstream so I'd like to understand which are the suggestion to keep this ingestion running as expected.

Thanks and regards,

i

entry.title: Best solutions/suggestions to bring SAP Datasphere data to SAP BW 7.5 on Oracle system
entry.content:
Dear SAP Colleagues & Experts

We are looking for best approach/suggestion to bring SAP Datasphere data to SAP BW 7.5 on Oracle system.

  1. DB connect can help to bring the data from Datasphere (Source) to SAP BW (Target)? If possible, in that case what are the per-requisite
  2. SAP Datasphere can place data to SAP BW 7.5 on Oracle file server path, further to create a datamodel 

Best regards,

Venkata

entry.title: How to transfer Object Ownership from One User to Another User in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
HI All,

How to transfer Object Ownership from One User to Another User in SAP Datasphere.

Couple of associates moved out of the project ,hence we would liek to transfer those objects which are created or changed by them to new users in SAP Datasphere.

1.DO we need to transfer object ownership from one user to another user.If yes, then can you please share me the step by step process.( I checked but do not find any option for Object Transfer)

2.If we do not transfer and de-activate or delete the user then what happened? Will objects will not work ?

3.If any objects which are scheduled by those users , hope those flows ( Background jobs/flows) will not schedule.

Can you please advise, thankyou

entry.title: Clarification on SAP Datasphere Capacity Units (CU) Usage, Limits, and Monitoring
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am currently investigating Capacity Unit (CU) usage on SAP Datasphere for a system. Due to limited internal resources, I would appreciate support from the community to clarify several points regarding calculation, display, and usage control mechanisms.
1. Current usage is about 45.6% based on the chart. What happens if usage exceeds 100% within a month? How will the overage be handled in terms of cost?
2. Is it possible to set up a notification when daily usage exceeds 200 CU?
3. Is it possible to enforce a hard limit to cap usage at 250 CU per day?
4. Is it possible to trigger a notification when monthly usage exceeds 70%?
5. Can we enforce a hard limit to prevent usage exceeding 1300 CU per month?
6. Where can these settings be configured (SAP Datasphere, SAP BTP, or SAP for Me)?
7. How can we view BDC (Business Data Cloud) and DSP (Datasphere) information in SAP for Me?
  • Is it possible to view detailed CU usage per service?
  • What roles/authorizations are required to access this information?

 

Thank you for your answer.

QuocDT5

entry.title: integrate MY SQL database to datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Sap team,We are going to connect MYSQl 5.7(version) database to BDC datasphere.
We are having windows server(Operating system) 2025 version
we would like to install SAP HANA SDI 2.0 -->dp agent SP 08 Patch 13(2.8.13) for remote tables flow from MySQL 5.7 source to bdc datasphere.
Can you please suggest us which dp agent version is compatible with windows OS and MySQL 5.7 db.

entry.title: Restricted KYF in Datasphere using hierarchy nodes with Hierarchy with directory
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to create a P&L in SAC with Analytical model in Datasphere. I am using Hierarchy with Directory for the GLAccount Dimension, and I would like to create the P&L Structure in Datasphere using restricted KYF. The KYF need to be restricted for selected Nodes from the Hierarchy.

What would be the way to go?

I would appreciate it if someone could point me in the right direction.


KR,
Tomas

entry.title: what is consumer type SQLS in transaction DHRDBMON ?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

by looking at DHRDBMON I found a new consumer type called

SQLS - SQL Replication Procedures

From what I understand and remember:

APE => Datasphere's Replication Flows.

BDC => BDC standard data products (APRS)

what is SQLS and how it works? 

is this some sort of replication with the channel based on ABAP SQL service ?

are there any documentation on how to setup and use this?

Thanks,

Alberto

entry.title: SAC and Datasphere on the same datacenter
entry.content: I see that SAP has a prerequisite stating that SAC and Datasphere must be on the same data center for Seamless Planning to work. However, in my project, it seems to be working even though they are hosted on different data centers. May I know how it works?

entry.title: Scoped Role Migration from One Tenant to Another Tenant
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

As our Datasphere environments keep on growing with more spaces being created to address security concerns, to ensure security setup is properly isolating correct user groups, we are assigning scoped roles on the spaces. With Environment exponentially growing with more spaces, it is becoming exponentially difficult to maintain Scoped roles environment wise. the lack of automation injects the Possibility of Human error to maintain it for each tenant  amounting to higher effort than it should take.

 

Hence, the question is - do we have any mechanism to transport Scoped roles from One Tenant to Another? If yes, can you advise any KBA? If no, do we have any influence request already in place? 

Kindly advise,

IBM

entry.title: SAP DataSphere | Asking about CU Limitation and Notification
entry.content:
Dear SAP Expert, 

Currently, we are starting to use SAP Datasphere and would like to monitor the system's Capacity Unit (CU) consumption. We also need to define limits and set up alerts in case usage exceeds the amount we have purchased.
However, based on our research, we have not found any SAP-released features related to CU limitation or notification available in the SAP Datasphere Portal (UI).
We would like to ask whether there are any functions in the SAP Datasphere Portal that allow us to configure CU usage limits and set up alerts.
If these features are not currently available, does SAP have a roadmap to develop such capabilities for SAP Datasphere?
We kindly request SAP to review and provide us with detailed guidance on this matter.

Thank you so much,

entry.title: Lack of History Tracking for Key Figure Changes in Object Store and HANA DB (SAP Datasphere)
entry.content:
We are working on a data model in SAP Datasphere where business data is being ingested into both:

  • Object Store (Data Lake / Files)
  • SAP HANA Cloud (local tables / persisted models)

In our use case, key figures (measures) are subject to updates and corrections during data submissions and transformations.

However, we observe that:

  • Changes to key figures are overwritten
  • There is no standard history tracking/versioning mechanism
  • This limitation exists in both:
    • Object Store-based datasets
    • HANA DB persisted tables inside Datasphere

entry.title: Datasphere - Scheduling task chains skipping holidays
entry.content:
In Datasphere.
Is there an option to schedule a Task Chain skipping holidays?

Could be possible to implement the schedule outside Datasphere (enterprise scheduler, or custom script) to apply holiday calendars, and then call the Datasphere task chain?

entry.title: For CDC-enabled CDS Extraction replicated to AWS s3, when /1DH/OPERATION is unsupported types
entry.content: For CDC-enabled CDS Extraction replicated to AWS s3 through Replication flow, when /1DH/OPERATIONis excluded as an unsupported datatype, what is the supported mechanism to identify delete records in the Outbound dataset (In SAP BDC Outbound Premium Integration)?
